svg.text: A Population Genomics Data Manager's Year
The recurring cycle of merge, filter, convert, annotate, repeat
200 samples, whole genome, from sequencing facility
Per-sample: het rate, call rate, depth, sex check, duplicates
QUAL, depth, missingness, HWE, MAF, biallelic SNPs only
Assign labels from metadata, verify with PCA/admixture
Consequence, impact, gene, protein change, SIFT, PolyPhen
ClinVar, dbSNP rsIDs, CADD scores, gnomAD frequencies
Each format requires a different script. Each must be re-run after any upstream change.
Different subset for each tool: biallelic only, specific populations, MAF thresholds, regions...
Allele frequencies, SFS, nucleotide diversity (pi), Tajima's D, LD decay, het rates...
Generate reports for PI, collaborators, grant reviews
Must re-merge with Batch 1, regenerate the merged VCF from scratch
on the new merged file (thresholds may change with more samples)
VEP/ClinVar on the new merged VCF
PLINK, EIGENSTRAT, TreeMix, SFS, Beagle, BED...
"Send me just the African samples in PLINK format, biallelic only"
"Repeat with MAF > 0.01 instead of 0.05" -- regenerate everything
New ClinVar release quarterly: must re-annotate entire VCF (96+ seconds)
GRCh37 to GRCh38: remap coordinates, re-annotate, re-export all formats
Each triggers the full merge-filter-annotate-convert-analyze cycle again
The questions no one can answer:
"Which VCF did I use for that TreeMix run in March?"
"Were the soft-deleted samples excluded from the SFS I sent to Dr. Smith?"
"What filters were applied to the PLINK file for the GWAS?"
"Can you re-create the EIGENSTRAT file from January but with updated ClinVar?"
"A student left. Their directory has 47 VCF files. Which is the final one?"
"The reviewer wants us to prove we used the same samples across all analyses."
Over a typical project lifetime: 5-15 merge cycles, 50+ format conversions, 200+ intermediate files,
and zero systematic provenance tracking. This is the problem GraphMana solves.
